summary_text: **Findings (severity-ordered)** 1. **Critical: Body source is inconsistent across runtimes (Python vs Worker).** Python queue loader uses `Body` (fallback `Title`) for `text` in [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L270), while Worker uses `Body JA` (fallback `Body`) in [notion-client.ts](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/worker/src/notion-client.ts#L98). Writes currently target `Body` in [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L328). **Drift effect:** same Notion row can produce different post text depending on runtime path. **Fix idea:** define one canonical field contract (e.g., `Body` only, or explicit priority), enforce it in both loaders, and add parity tests with mixed `Body/Body JA` fixtures. 2. **High: Local schedule repairs are easily overwritten by Notion SoT unless dirty-marked.** `queue_audit --fix` rewrites `scheduled_for` in [queue_audit.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/queue_audit.py#L374) but does not set `_schedule_changed_at`. Scheduler preserves local schedule only when dirty in [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L417), and merge logic has same rule in [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L452). Behavior is explicitly tested in [test_scheduler_r11_r12.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py#L452) and [test_scheduler_r11_r12.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_scheduler_r11_r12.py#L460). **Drift effect:** local schedule “repairs” disappear on next Notion reload. **Fix idea:** when queue_audit changes schedule, also set `_schedule_changed_at` and optionally trigger immediate `push_updates_to_notion`. 3. **High: Notion-first reload can overwrite local repairs for Title/Text/Image/thumbnail flags.** Scheduler rebuilds queue from Notion in [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L399), only supplementing `image_path/reply_url` when missing in [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L410), plus dirty operational fields in [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L415). Notion mapping loads title/text/thumbnail/image in [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L266), [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L270), [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L274), [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L278), [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L292). **Drift effect:** local-only edits to those fields are non-durable unless also written to Notion. **Fix idea:** add explicit content dirty flags + push path, or require all repairs to use Notion patch scripts first, then reload local. 4. **High: Two queue loaders use different merge semantics.** Generic loader [post_generator.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L264) preserves only `image_path/reply_url` in [post_generator.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L283), while scheduler loader preserves dirty operational fields too in [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L415). **Drift effect:** generator/autogen and scheduler can observe different effective queue state for same Notion/local data. **Fix idea:** centralize merge policy in one shared function and use it in both code paths. 5. **Medium: Empty Notion response is treated as authoritative immediately.** Scheduler sets `posts = []` on empty Notion result in [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L424). Generic loader returns empty Notion list as-is in [post_generator.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L286). **Drift effect:** transient Notion query/schema/filter issues can temporarily “drain” runtime queue view. **Fix idea:** add a circuit-breaker (e.g., require consecutive empty responses or compare against prior non-terminal count before trusting empty). 6. **Medium: `scheduled_for` normalization differs across runtimes.** Python normalizes Notion datetime to local canonical format in [post_generator.p...
